data.title: Calian Group Non-GAAP EPS of $0.93
data.description:
• Calian Group press release (OTCPK:CLNFF): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.93.
  • Revenue at $194 million
  • Gross margin at 33.4%
  • Adjusted EBITDA1 of $17 million
  • Operating free cash flow1 of $10 million
  • Very strong signings of $248 million
  • Growth in our defence end market solutions of 13%
  • Since the launch of the NCIB, the Company repurchased 416,812 shares, or 4% of the float, in consideration of $19.7 million
  • Increasing NCIB - plan to repurchase up to 6% of float in FY25
  • Guidance withdrawn due to ongoing economic and geopolitical uncertainty as well as limited visibility and timing of key opportunities in the ITCS segment

data.title: Endava Non-GAAP EPS of £0.34, revenue of £194.8M; initiates Q4 outlook
data.description:
• Endava press release (NYSE:DAVA): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of £0.34.
  • Revenue of £194.8M (+11.7% Y/Y).
  • Revenue increase at constant currency (a non-IFRS measure)* was 12.4% for Q3 FY2025.
  • Net cash from operating activities was £18.7 million in Q3 FY2025, compared to net cash from operating activities of £3.0 million in the same period in the prior year.
  • Adjusted free cash flow (a non-IFRS measure)* was £17.5 million in Q3 FY2025, compared to £2.2 million in the same period in the prior year.
  • At March 31, 2025, Endava had cash and cash equivalents of £68.3 million, compared to £62.4 million at June 30, 2024.
  • Number of clients with over £1 million in revenue on a rolling twelve-month basis was 136 at March 31, 2025, compared to 142 clients at March 31, 2024.
  • Top 10 clients accounted for 39% of revenue in Q3 FY2025, compared to 34% in the same period in the prior year.

  • OUTLOOK:

    Fourth Quarter Fiscal Year 2025:

    Endava expects revenue will be in the range of £186.0 million to £188.0 million, representing a constant currency revenue change of between (1.0)% and 0.0% on a year over year basis. Endava expects adjusted diluted EPS to be in the range of £0.22 to £0.24 per share.

    Full Fiscal Year 2025:

    Endava expects revenue will be in the range of £771.5 million to £773.5 million, representing a constant currency revenue increase of between 6.0% and 6.5% on a year over year basis. Endava expects adjusted diluted EPS to be in the range of £1.11 to £1.13 per share.

data.title: Franklin Street Properties to review options, including a possible sale
data.description:
Franklin Street Properties (NYSE:FSP) stock rose 5.5% in Wednesday premarket trading after the company's board started a review of the office REIT's strategic alternatives for ways to maximize shareholder value. The review will explore such options as the sale of assets, the sale of the company, and refinancing existing debt, it said on Wednesday.

"We believe that FSP's share price does not adequately reflect the underlying value of our real estate, and, accordingly, we have undertaken this strategic review process to explore opportunities to eliminate this disconnect," said Chairman and CEO George J. Carter.

In the past year, Franklin Street (NYSE:FSP) shares have dropped 24%, compared with the S&P 500's 13% increase.

The company has hired BofA Securities as its financial adviser in connection with the review. Franklin Street (FSP) gave no assurances regarding the outcome or a timetable for completion of the review process.

data.title: Kamada beats top-line estimates; reaffirms FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Kamada press release (NASDAQ:KMDA): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.07 in-line.
  • Revenue of $44M (+16.7% Y/Y) beats by $1.12M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA, as detailed in the tables below, was $11.6 million in the first quarter of 2025, up 54% from the $7.5 million reported in the first quarter of 2024.
  • Cash used in operating activities was $0.5 million in the first quarter of 2025, as compared to cash provided by operating activities of $1.0 million in the first quarter of 2024.
  • As of March 31, 2025, the company had cash and cash equivalents of $76.3 million, as compared to $78.4 million as of December 31, 2024.
  • Fiscal 2025 GuidanceKamada continues to expect to generate fiscal year 2025 total revenues in the range of $178 million to $182 million vs. $182.62M consensus, and adjusted EBITDA in the range of $38 million to $42 million, representing a year-over-year increase of approximately 12% in revenues and 17% in adjusted EBITDA based on the mid-point of the 2025 guidance.

data.title: Bitfarms GAAP EPS of -$0.07 misses by $0.04, revenue of $66.85M beats by $1.89M
data.description:
• Bitfarms press release (NASDAQ:BITF): Q1 GAAP EPS of -$0.07 misses by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $66.85M (+32.8% Y/Y) beats by $1.89M.
  • Gross mining margin of 43%, down from 63% from Q1 2024.
  • Total energy pipeline of ~1.4 GW, ~80% based in the U.S.
  • Current hashrate of 19.5 EHuM, up 200% from 6.5 EHuM as of March 31, 2024
  • Current efficiency of 19 w/TH, an improvement of 44% from 34 w/TH as of March 31, 2024.
  • Adjusted EBITDA* of $16 million, or 23% of revenue, down from $23 million or 46% of revenue in Q1 2024
  • The Company earned 693 BTC at an average direct cost of production per BTC* of $47,800
  • Total cash cost of production per BTC* was $72,300 in Q1 2025

data.title: Coca-Cola Europacific Partners updates guidance ahead of investor event
data.description:
Coca-Cola Europacific Partners plc (NASDAQ:CCEP) issued a guidance update ahead of the company's Capital Market Event in Manila.

The UK-based company reaffirmed its mid-term growth objectives for comparable FX-neutral revenue growth of ~4% and comparable operating profit growth of ~7%. CCEP also sees comparable free cash flow of at least €1.7 billion and a net debt-to-EBITDA ratio of 2.5X to 3.0X. Comparable ROIC growth of ~50bps per annum is anticipated. Capital expenditure spending is seen coming in at 4% to 5% of revenue. CCEP expects a dividend payout ratio of around 50%.

Chair Sol Daurella, Chief Executive Damian Gammell, and members of Coca-Cola Europacific Partners' (NASDAQ:CCEP) executive leadership team plan to present the company's growth strategy and key enablers to drive the delivery of the mid-term growth objectives. The presentation will also include how the company is scaling the business across multiple key capabilities and accelerating productivity through technology.

Shares of Coca-Cola Europacific Partners plc (CCEP) are up 13.8% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: UnitedHealth approves $1M annual base salary for new CEO
data.description:
A day after announcing the abrupt departure of its previous CEO, Andrew Witty, due to personal reasons, UnitedHealth Group (NYSE:UNH) disclosed on Wednesday that its new chief executive officer, Stephen Hemsley, will receive an annual base salary of $1M.

The managed care giant added that its board and its compensation and human resources committee approved the compensation package for Hemsley “after considering market data, advice from the committee’s independent compensation consultant, and other factors.”

Hemsley, 72, who was previously the company’s chairman of the board and a former CEO from 2016 to 2027, was brought to the company’s leadership effective immediately after the sudden departure of Andrew Witty on Monday.

Despite serving a dual role as the company’s chairman of the board and its CEO, Hemsley will no longer receive compensation as a board director. Eden Prairie, Minnesota-based UnitedHealth (NYSE:UNH) added.

A former CEO of British drugmaker GSK (GSK), Witty, 60, who joined UNH as the head of its pharmacy benefit manager unit Optum RX in 2018 and became the company’s CEO in 2021, drew $1.5M of base salary in 2024, according to a recent analysis from Fierce Healthcare.
